title: From scroll to codex — how the book form developed
sources: Cyril Davenport, The Book — Its History and Development (1907) — full historical arc from tablets to printed book
Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — preface on the binding tradition
notes: Historical reading article covering: wax tablets, papyrus rolls, the transition to parchment codex in the first centuries AD, Coptic binding as a transition structure, the spread of the codex through monastic culture. Bookbinding follows, not leads, the codex form. Readers who make their own books understand the form they are working in.
